question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Field: stream = new StreamProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamProperties.maxSubscribeAttempts=3 threshold to cause a legitimate block node to be permanently marked inactive by sending exactly 3 malformed responses, under the precondition that the attacker can intercept or spoof block node responses, causing the importer to switch to an attacker-controlled node that injects freeze transactions, permanently freezing victim funds?
